day_summary: No games on tonight's schedule. Nothing needs you right now.

agent_updates.title: Rebound Matchup Adjustments Implemented
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **The Finding:** We were missing the mark on rebounds because we were completely ignoring head-to-head size differences and who is actually boxing out who.

**The Fix:** I built the **Rebound Matchup Model**. It uses the new matchups feed feed to dynamically fetch the expected defender for every player on the slate. It scales each player's proj_reb based on two things: 1) the defender's historical rebound rate, and 2) the height advantage/disadvantage (in inches).

**Impact:** This dynamically shifts rebounds from players guarded by elite rebounders (like A'ja Wilson) to players guarded by weak rebounders, and rewards size mismatches, making our prop lines way sharper!

agent_updates.title: Sharper minutes forecasts for players changing roles
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **The finding:** We compared two ways to forecast playing time on the games that matter most — players stepping into bigger or different roles.

**Result:** the simpler rolling-average method is **better by 0.86 minutes** on these role-change games. The role-aware version systematically over-corrected on promoted or demoted players.

**Action:** we kept the rolling-average method as the official minutes model and shelved the role-aware one.

agent_updates.title: Historical Era Adjustments (2024-2026)
agent_updates.body_markdown: > 2026 season represents a distinct era in officiating. Free Throw Rate skyrocketed from to 0.315 adding over 3 free throw attempts per team per game. Impact on Backtesting When backtesting player stat projections (especially PTS) on 2024 2025 data we must apply a downward era-adjustment for free throw volume to avoid artificially grading our 2026-calibrated models too harshly on historical undershoots.

floor.label: 99 things are lined up in the work queue — a healthy runway (we keep at least 40 ready). This queue view is 4d old — a refresh is due.

upcoming.note: These are the next things the system will work on — what each will be and why it matters. Tap to move an item to the top, bump it up, push it down, or shelf it. Items waiting on your call are parked until you answer — then they jump to the top.

reports.placeholder: Tonight's game reports land at 5:15pm ET.

rollover.label: rollover armed for tomorrow ✓
